name: Wallace, Bill
resolved_employer: Unidentified nonprofit organization (self-reported occupation and employer both 'Nonprofit'), Tyler, TX
evidence: No public profile could be corroborated for a Bill/William Wallace in Tyler, TX 75701 working in the nonprofit sector. Searches surfaced only same-named individuals elsewhere (a Bill Wallace at Success North Dallas in Addison, and a Bill Wallace who is Chief Administrative Officer at Central Health in Travis County) - neither matches the Tyler 75701 address, so neither was used. Classification is an employer-field fallback only: the filing's self-reported occupation/employer 'Nonprofit' is the sole basis. FEC individual-contribution and Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist searches returned no match for this person.

name: Orton, Gregg
resolved_employer: National Council of Asian Pacific Americans (NCAPA) - National Director; NCAPA is fiscally sponsored as a project of the Tides Center
evidence: Gregg Orton is National Director of NCAPA, a Washington, DC-based coalition of ~45 national Asian American and Native Hawaiian/Pacific Islander organizations; NCAPA operates as a project of the Tides Center, exactly matching the filed employer string 'Tides center/ncapa' and the Washington DC 20011 zip. He previously spent nearly a decade on Capitol Hill for Rep. Al Green (D-TX), serving as his Chief of Staff. Full bio read found one additional role: Advisory Board member, Friends of the National Asian Pacific American Museum. No Texas lobbyist registration and no FEC PAC contribution records were found.

affiliations.org: National Council of Asian Pacific Americans (NCAPA)
affiliations.snippet: "Gregg Orton serves as National Director, overseeing policy development and communications strategy" for NCAPA, a coalition of national AA and NHPI organizations organized around committees on civil rights, education, health, housing and economic justice, immigration, and civic engagement. NCAPA operates as a project of the Tides Center.

affiliations.org: Friends of the National Asian Pacific American Museum
affiliations.snippet: Listed on the organization's team page as an Advisory Board member, identified as National Director of The National Council of Asian Pacific Americans.

name: Jacobs, Gwen
resolved_employer: Law Office of Eric Harron, Austin, TX - criminal defense attorney
evidence: Corroborated as Laura Gwendolyn 'Gwen' Jacobs, Texas bar number 24066677, licensed December 11, 2008 (J.D., St. Mary's University, 2008), practicing criminal law at The Law Office of Eric Harron, 819 1/2 West 11th Street, Austin, TX - an exact match to the filed employer and Austin location. Directory listings confirm she practices in criminal, DWI, and ADR matters. Full profile read surfaced no board seats, policy-advocacy roles, or organizational memberships. No FEC PAC contributions and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found.

name: Penarredonda, Ulises
resolved_employer: CREAN - Defense Manufacturing Subject Matter Expert (professional-profile listing); previously Divisional CPI (Continuous Process Improvement) Coordinator, U.S. Navy, Norfolk, VA
evidence: The filing carries no occupation or employer. The only public individual matching this distinctive surname in the Washington DC metro is Ulises Guillermo Penarredonda, a property owner of record in Alexandria/Fairfax County, VA, whose professional profile lists him as a Defense Manufacturing Subject Matter Expert at CREAN and a former Divisional Continuous Process Improvement Coordinator with the U.S. Navy in Norfolk, VA; his profile now shows an Austin, TX location, which is consistent with a Northern Virginia address on a 2022-dated contribution followed by an Austin move. Identity is corroborated by surname rarity plus the Alexandria VA property record, but not by occupation on the filing, so confidence is capped at medium. His Navy work is federal civil/contract service rather than a defense-contractor ownership, executive, board, or lobbying role, so no military_defense affiliation is recorded. No FEC PAC contributions and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found.

name: Aarons, Stephen
resolved_employer: Traverse Legal, PLC (Austin, TX office) - corporate and securities attorney
evidence: Stephen M. Aarons is a corporate and securities attorney in Traverse Legal PLC's Austin, Texas office, an exact match to the filed employer, occupation, and city. Texas bar number 24092008, licensed 11/06/2014; J.D. Tulane (2014), B.S. Finance, University of Maryland Smith School (2006). His practice covers private securities offerings, venture capital financings, M&A, and corporate governance, and he has served as outside general counsel to hedge, private equity, real estate, and venture capital funds; before law school he spent five years in banking and real estate finance at Wells Fargo Private Bank and Wentwood Capital Advisors, a real estate investment management firm. Full firm-bio read found no board seats, policy-advocacy roles, or organizational memberships beyond law-school honors (Moot Court Board, Order of Barristers). The pipeline records 7 prior FEC donations with a fully Democratic partisan lean; direct FEC.gov and OpenSecrets record retrieval was blocked (403 / JavaScript-only search UI), so no specific federal committee or PAC recipient could be independently sourced and none is asserted here. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found.

Pipeline FEC-match metadata shows 13 prior federal donations with a 0.90 Democratic partisan lean, consistent with a routine small-dollar federal donor, but no specific FEC record was independently retrieved (fec.gov result pages are JS-rendered and the OpenFEC API returned HTTP 429; OpenSecrets returned HTTP 403).

name: Kane, Jennifer

evidence: Identity corroborated on three points: Psychology Today lists Jennifer Kane, Clinical Social Work/Therapist, Greenwood Village, CO 80111 — an exact zip match to the donor record; occupation 'Therapist' and employer 'Self' match a solo private practice; her practice site lists her office at 6200 S. Syracuse Way, Greenwood Village CO 80111, Colorado LCSW license CSW.09925689. Full About-page read shows a solo clinician (Emory BA/MA 1982, University of Denver MSW 1998) with training in EFT, CBT, MBSR, EMDR and Discernment Therapy; no board seats, corporate roles, or policy-advocacy positions disclosed. name: Brooks, Deon
resolved_employer: Third Coast Environmental Services, Inc. — founder/owner; state-certified drinking-water sampling contractor for TCEQ
evidence: A Voyage Houston profile identifies Deon Brooks as founder/owner of Third Coast Environmental Services, a Houston-area company he started in 2007 that collects drinking-water samples under the State Drinking Water Contract for the Texas Commission on Environmental Quality, grown to ~12 employees across 70+ Texas counties. This matches the filing's occupation 'Water treatment' and employer 'Self' (business owner). Confidence is medium rather than high because the company address is Houston 77041 while the donor zip is Katy 77493 — a plausible same-metro home/work split, but not independently confirmed. Full profile read shows no board seats or policy-advocacy roles; a possible Sam Houston Water Association appearance was too weakly sourced to record. No FEC PAC contributions and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found; pipeline metadata shows zero matched federal donations.

name: Kearse, Kheperah
resolved_employer: LIFE Camp, Inc. (Love Ignites Freedom thru Education) — Chief Wellness Officer; also founder of Gemnasium LLC
evidence: Robin 'Kheperah' Kearse is the Chief Wellness Officer of LIFE Camp, Inc., the South Jamaica, Queens violence-prevention/intervention nonprofit named in the donor's employer field; the filed occupation 'CWO' matches her documented title exactly, and she has a NY/NJ-area professional footprint consistent with the Englewood NJ zip. She co-created culturally-adaptive healing and trauma-response methodologies used across the NYC Crisis Management System and founded Gemnasium LLC, a personal/professional wellbeing practice.

affiliations.snippet: LIFE Camp, founded April 2002, describes itself as 'one of the leading violence prevention and intervention organizations in America'; Kheperah Kearse is listed as Chief Wellness Officer, 'a wellness apostle whose commitment is to normalizing the mind-body wellness lifestyle within the most underserved communities in the US.' LIFE Camp is a community gun-violence-interruption organization within the NYC Crisis Management System. Note: this is community violence intervention/service delivery rather than legislative gun-control lobbying.

Kheperah is the founder of Gemnasium LLC, a model which helps people discover their own pathway to personal and professional well-being, using transformative learning, nutritional balance and emotional intelligence training.

name: Segura-Kelly, Daniel
evidence: Daniel Stephen Segura-Kelly (b. 1984) is an Austin/Travis County Democratic Party activist — a Travis County Democratic Party precinct chair and, per Austin Bulldog reporting, an officer of Stonewall Democrats of Austin. Identity corroborated by Austin residency, his own separate small-dollar TEC-reported donation to the Travis County Democratic Party, and a donation pattern (23 federal contributions, partisan lean 1.0) consistent with a party volunteer. No occupation or employer is listed on the filing and no employment record could be located in public sources, so no industry is assigned. Note: his roles are volunteer/party positions, not employment.

affiliations.role: Precinct chair (precinct number reported variously as 101 and 126) and Finance Committee co-chair
affiliations.snippet: Austin Bulldog reporting identifies Daniel Segura-Kelly as a Travis County Democratic Party precinct chair and co-chair of the party's Finance Committee, describing his attempt at an April 19 committee meeting to have members hear a recording related to allegations against party leadership. (Direct page fetch returned HTTP 403; content obtained via search index of this URL.)
Same Austin Bulldog reporting describes Segura-Kelly as Vice President of Stonewall Democrats of Austin. Not independently confirmed on stonewallaustin.org; treat as single-source.

evidence: Hilary Rand Whitfield is a longtime Austin, TX resident (matches 78704) and a volunteer state leader for the Texas chapter of Moms Demand Action for Gun Sense in America, the grassroots arm of Everytown for Gun Safety; she testified before Texas legislative committees on gun safety bills in 2019 and is quoted in Everytown/Moms Demand Action press releases. Her self-reported employer (Everytown for Gun Safety) and Austin location both corroborate.

evidence: Alexis Anderson-Reed is President/CEO of State Voices, a national progressive civic-engagement and voter-mobilization network she has led since 2018, and sits on the boards of the Alliance for Youth Organizing, the State Innovation Exchange, and the Scherman Foundation.
